{"content": "     1|# jref - JSON Reference CLI
     2|
     3|A lightweight CLI tool to interact with "condensed" JSON project snapshots. Designed for both human developers and AI agents.
     4|
     5|## Features
     6|
     7|- **Inspect** - View directoryStructure and metadata without loading entire file
     8|- **Search** - High-speed regex or keyword searching across all file entries
     9|- **Extract** - Unpack specific files, directories, or entire project to filesystem
    10|- **Query** - Get content of a specific file path (AI-friendly)
    11|- **Reconstruct** - Dry-run mode to verify if local directory matches snapshot
    12|- **UI** - Interactive Terminal User Interface for browsing snapshots (mobile-friendly)
    13|- **Patch** - Update/add files and metadata in a snapshot via stdin or args
    14|- **Serve** - Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for agentic interoperability
    15|- **Diff** - Compare snapshot against local filesystem
    16|- **Pack** - Native creation of snapshots from local directories (.gitignore support)
    17|- **Summarize** - Generate token-efficient architectural maps (signatures only)
    18|
    19|## Target Environments
    20|
    21|- Termux (Android/ARM)
    22|- Raspberry Pi 4 (Linux/ARM)
    23|- Standard x86 Linux
    24|
    25|## Installation
    26|
    27|```bash
    28|# From source
    29|npm install
    30|npm run build
    31|
    32|# Link globally
    33|npm link
    34|
    35|# Or install directly
    36|npm install -g jref
    37|```
    38|
    39|## Quick Start
    40|
    41|```bash
    42|# Create a snapshot from current directory
    43|jref pack . > snapshot.json
    44|
    45|# Inspect a snapshot
    46|jref inspect snapshot.json
    47|
    48|# Interactive browsing (mobile-friendly)
    49|jref ui snapshot.json
    50|
    51|# Search for patterns
    52|jref search "function" snapshot.json
    53|
    54|# Compare snapshot to local disk
    55|jref diff snapshot.json
    56|
    57|# Start MCP server for agents
    58|jref serve snapshot.json
    59|```
    60|
    61|## Data Schema
    62|
    63|jref works with JSON snapshots following this schema:
    64|
    65|```json
    66|{
    67|  "directoryStructure": "String representing the file tree",
    68|  "files": {
    69|    "path/to/file1.ts": "file contents...",
    70|    "path/to/file2.ts": "file contents..."
    71|  },
    72|  "instruction": "Optional context or AI instructions",
    73|  "fileSummary": "Optional summary of files",
    74|  "userProvidedHeader": "Optional custom header"
    75|}
    76|```
    77|
    78|## Commands
    79|
    80|### pack
    81|
    82|Create a jref-compliant JSON snapshot from a local directory.
    83|
    84|```bash
    85|jref pack [directory] [options]
    86|
    87|Options:
    88|  --instruction <text>  Add custom AI instructions
    89|  --summary <text>      Add a high-level file summary
    90|```
    91|
    92|**Examples:**
    93|```bash
    94|jref pack . > project.json
    95|jref pack src/utils --instruction "Follow TDD" > utils.json
    96|```
    97|
    98|### patch
    99|
   100|Update or add files and metadata in a snapshot without extraction.
   101|
   102|```bash
   103|jref patch [path] [content] [file.json]
   104|
   105|Options:
   106|  --instruction <text>  Update snapshot instructions
   107|  --summary <text>      Update snapshot summary
   108|```
   109|
   110|**Examples:**
   111|```bash
   112|# Update from args
   113|jref patch src/main.ts "new code" project.json > updated.json
   114|
   115|# Update from stdin
   116|cat fix.ts | jref patch src/main.ts project.json > updated.json
   117|
   118|# Update metadata only
   119|jref patch --instruction "New rules" project.json > updated.json
   120|```
   121|
   122|### diff
   123|
   124|Compare snapshot contents against the local filesystem.
   125|
   126|```bash
   127|jref diff [options] [file.json]
   128|
   129|Options:
   130|  --directory, -d <dir>   Target directory to compare against
   131|  --all, -a               Find extra local files not in snapshot
   132|```
   133|
   134|**Examples:**
   135|```bash
   136|jref diff snapshot.json
   137|jref diff --directory ./my-app snapshot.json
   138|```
   139|
   140|### serve
   141|
   142|Start a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server to expose the snapshot to AI agents.
   143|
   144|```bash
   145|jref serve [file.json]
   146|```
   147|
   148|**MCP Tools Exposed:**
   149|- `inspect`: Get metadata and structure
   150|- `search`: Regex search across files
   151|- `query`: Read specific file content
   152|
   153|### summarize
   154|
   155|Generate a token-efficient architectural map by stripping implementation details.
   156|
   157|```bash
   158|jref summarize [file.json]
   159|```
   160|
   161|**Implementation:** Strips function/class bodies, leaving only signatures and imports.
   162|
   163|### ui
   164|
   165|Interactive Terminal User Interface for browsing project snapshots.
   166|
   167|```bash
   168|jref ui [file]
   169|
   170|Controls:
   171|  ↑↓ Arrows / j,k Navigate tree/file
   172|  ←→ Arrows / h,l Expand/collapse directories
   173|  Enter           Select file (view) or toggle directory
   174|  /               Search files by name
   175|  y               Yank (copy) current file content to clipboard
   176|  e               Edit current file in-memory using $EDITOR
   177|  c               Toggle compact mode
   178|  Esc             Back/Exit
   179|
   180|Keybinds for Mobile/Termux:
   181|- Yank (y): Uses `termux-clipboard-set` if available.
   182|- Edit (e): Spawns your local editor (vi, nano, etc.) for temporary edits.
   183|```
   184|
   185|### inspect
   186|
   187|View directoryStructure and metadata without loading entire file.
   188|
   189|```bash
   190|jref inspect [options] [file]
   191|
   192|Options:
   193|  --metadata, -m     Show only metadata
   194|  --structure, -s    Show only directory structure
   195|  --files, -f        Show only file list
   196|  --summary          Show instruction/summary/header
   197|```
   198|
   199|### search
   200|
   201|High-speed regex or keyword searching across all entries.
   202|
   203|```bash
   204|jref search <pattern> [options] [file]
   205|```
   206|
   207|### query
   208|
   209|Get content of a specific file path. Optimized for AI agents.
   210|
   211|```bash
   212|jref query --path <path> [file]
   213|```
   214|
   215|## AI Agent Usage
   216|
   217|jref is designed for agentic workflows. Use `--json` for structured output or `--raw` for pure content.
   218|
   219|```bash
   220|# Get summarized map for context
   221|jref summarize project.json > map.json
   222|
   223|# Use MCP in agent-enabled IDEs
   224|jref serve project.json
   225|```
   226|
   227|## License
   228|
   229|MIT

{"content": "     1|# jref Agent Instructions
     2|
     3|## Core Capabilities for Agents
     4|
     5|jref is optimized for LLM agents working in mobile or Linux environments.
     6|
     7|- **MCP Protocol**: Run `jref serve snapshot.json` to expose the codebase as a suite of tool-calling endpoints (metadata, search, query).
     8|- **Surgical Patching**: Use `jref patch` to modify files in a snapshot without full extraction. Supports piping code fixes directly.
     9|- **Architectural Mapping**: Use `jref summarize` to get a signature-only map of a large codebase, saving context tokens.
    10|- **Verification**: Use `jref diff` to verify if your suggested changes in a snapshot match the local filesystem.
    11|
    12|## Command Reference
    13|
    14|| Command | Usage | Purpose |
    15|| :--- | :--- | :--- |
    16|| `pack` | `jref pack [dir]` | Create snapshot from local files |
    17|| `patch` | `jref patch [path] [content]` | Update snapshot content/metadata |
    18|| `summarize` | `jref summarize [file]` | Strip implementation details for context |
    19|| `serve` | `jref serve [file]` | Start MCP server (stdio) |
    20|| `diff` | `jref diff [file]` | Compare snapshot vs local disk |
    21|| `inspect` | `jref inspect [file]` | Get metadata and ASCII tree |
    22|| `search` | `jref search [pattern]` | Regex search across all files |
    23|| `query` | `jref query --path [p]` | Read specific file content |
    24|
    25|## Mobile/Termux Optimization
    26|
    27|- **Clipboard**: The TUI supports `y` keybind to yank content using `termux-clipboard-set`.
    28|- **Editor**: The TUI supports `e` keybind to spawn `$EDITOR` (vi/nano).
    29|- **Streaming**: Large JSON files are handled via chunked processing to avoid OOM on mobile hardware.
    30|
    31|## Snapshot Schema
    32|
    33|```json
    34|{
    35|  "directoryStructure": "ASCII tree string",
    36|  "files": {"path/to/file.ts": "source code..."},
    37|  "instruction": "AI system prompt context",
    38|  "fileSummary": "Project high-level overview"
    39|}
    40|```
    41|
    42|## Agent Workflows
    43|
    44|### 1. Context Injection
    45|```bash
    46|# Get signature map for initial planning
    47|jref summarize project.json > map.json
    48|```
    49|
    50|### 2. State Mutation
    51|```bash
    52|# Apply a fix suggested by the LLM
    53|cat fix.ts | jref patch src/main.ts project.json > updated.json
    54|```
    55|
    56|### 3. Tool Interoperability
    57|Connect your agent runner to `node dist/index.js serve project.json` to enable live browsing.

{"path": "./docs/jref.md", "content": "# jref — JSON Reference CLI

## Table of Contents

1. [Overview](#overview)
2. [Features](#features)
3. [Installation](#installation)
4. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
5. [Global Options](#global-options)
6. [Command Reference](#command-reference)

   - [inspect — View Snapshot Metadata](#inspect)

   - [query — Retrieve Specific File Content](#query)
   - [extract — Unpack Files to Filesystem](#extract)
   - [reconstruct — Verify Local vs Snapshot](#reconstruct)
   - [diff — Compare Snapshot to Local Filesystem](#diff)
   - [patch — Modify Snapshots Without Extraction](#patch)
   - [summarize — Generate Architectural Maps](#summarize)
   - [serve — MCP Server for AI Agents](#serve)
   - [ui — Interactive TUI Browser](#ui)
7. [Snapshot Schema](#snapshot-schema)
8. [AI Agent Workflows](#ai-agent-workflows)
9. [Target Environments](#target-environments)
10. [Use Cases](#use-cases)
11. [Exit Codes](#exit-codes)
12. [File Size and Streaming Behavior](#file-size-and-streaming-behavior)

---

## Overview

**jref** (JSON Reference) is a CLI tool for creating, inspecting, searching, and manipulating **condensed JSON project snapshots** — single JSON files that pack an entire codebase's file tree and file contents into one portable, streamable artifact. It is designed for both human developers and AI agents working in constrained environments (mobile, ARM, low-memory).

A snapshot captures:
- An ASCII directory tree (`directoryStructure`)
- All source file contents keyed by path (`files`)
- Optional AI instructions (`instruction`)
- Optional high-level project summary (`fileSummary`)
- Optional custom user header (`userProvidedHeader`)

---

## Features

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **pack** | Create a snapshot from a local directory (respects `.gitignore`) |
| **inspect** | View tree and metadata without loading the whole file into memory |
| **search** | High-speed regex / keyword search across all files in the snapshot |
| **query** | Pull out a single file's content by path (AI-friendly, supports `--raw`) |
| **extract** | Unpack specific files, directories, or the entire project to disk |
| **reconstruct** | Dry-run check: does the local directory match the snapshot? |
| **diff** | Compare snapshot contents against the live filesystem |
| **patch** | Update/add file contents or metadata in a snapshot without extracting |
| **summarize** | Strip implementation bodies, keep only signatures and imports |
| **serve** | Start an MCP (Model Context Protocol) stdio server for AI agents |
| **ui** | Interactive Terminal UI (ink/React) for browsing snapshots |

### Global Flags

| Flag | Alias | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| `--json` | `-j` | Output structured JSON (for AI consumption) |
| `--silent` | `-s` | Suppress all decorative/progress output |
| `--raw` | `-r` | Raw output — no formatting, pure content |
| `--help` | `-h` | Show global help or command-specific help |
| `--version` | `-v` | Print version string |

All commands accept stdin pipe input, enabling patterns like:

```bash
cat snapshot.json | jref inspect
cat snapshot.json | jref search "TODO"
```

---

## Installation

### From Source

```bash
git clone <repo>
cd jref
npm install
npm run build

# Link globally
npm link

# Or install globally directly
npm install -g .
```

### Requirements

- **Node.js** >= 18.0.0
- **npm** >= 9.0.0

### Verifying Installation

```bash
jref --version
# jref v1.0.0

jref --help
```

---

## Quick Start

### Create a Snapshot

```bash
# Snapshot the current working directory
jref pack . > project.json

# Snapshot with AI instructions
jref pack ./my-project \
  --instruction "Follow these architectural rules" \
  --summary "REST API server with three route modules" \
  > project.json
```

### Inspect a Snapshot

```bash
# Full overview (metadata + tree + file list + summary)
jref inspect project.json

# Only the directory tree
jref inspect --structure project.json

# Only file list with sizes
jref inspect --files project.json

# Only metadata (file count, total size, etc.)
jref inspect --metadata project.json

# Pipe from stdin
cat project.json | jref inspect
```

### Search Within a Snapshot

```bash
# Literal keyword search (escapes regex metacharacters)
jref search "async function" project.json

# Regex search
jref search "class.*Controller" --regex project.json

# Case-insensitive
jref search "export" --case-insensitive project.json

# Limit results and show context lines
jref search "TODO" --max-results 50 --context 2 project.json

# JSON output (for AI agents)
jref search "function" --json project.json

# Pipe input
cat project.json | jref search "TODO"
```

### Query a Specific File

```bash
# Get file content (human-readable with header)
jref query --path "src/main.ts" project.json

# Raw output (pure content, ideal for AI parsing / piping to patch)
jref query --path "src/main.ts" --raw project.json

# Specific line range
jref query --path "src/main.ts" --line-start 10 --line-end 50 project.json

# JSON structured output
jref query --path "src/main.ts" --json project.json
```

### Extract Files

```bash
# Extract entire project to ./extracted/
jref extract project.json

# Extract to custom output directory
jref extract --output ./output project.json

# Extract only specific files
jref extract --paths src/main.ts src/utils.ts project.json

# Flat output (filename only, no directory structure)
jref extract --flat --output ./output project.json

# Dry run (preview what would be extracted)
jref extract --dry-run project.json

# Skip existing files (do not overwrite)
jref extract --output ./output project.json

# Overwrite existing files
jref extract --overwrite --output ./output project.json
```

### Verify Local vs Snapshot

```bash
# Check if current directory matches snapshot
jref reconstruct project.json

# Check against a specific directory
jref reconstruct --directory ./my-app project.json

# Verbose: show which files are missing, extra, or modified
jref reconstruct --verbose project.json

# JSON output
jref reconstruct --json project.json

# Ignore missing files (snapshot files not on disk)
jref reconstruct --ignore-missing project.json

# Ignore extra files (local files not in snapshot)
jref reconstruct --ignore-extra project.json
```

### Diff Snapshot Against Filesystem

```bash
# Compare snapshot to current directory
jref diff project.json

# Compare to a specific directory
jref diff --directory ./another-project project.json

# Include extra local files in diff output
jref diff --all project.json

# JSON structured output
jref diff --json project.json
```

### Patch a Snapshot (Surgical Edit)

```bash
# Update file content from argument
jref patch src/main.ts "new file content" project.json > updated.json

# Update file content from stdin (pipe a file's content in)
cat fix.ts | jref patch src/main.ts project.json > updated.json

# Update metadata only
jref patch --instruction "Updated: follow new linting rules" project.json > updated.json

# Update file summary
jref patch --summary "Refactored auth module" project.json > updated.json

# Update both file and metadata
jref patch src/main.ts "new content" \
  --instruction "Updated instructions" \
  project.json > updated.json

# Content detection: if second positional arg ends in .json it's treated
# as the snapshot file path, not content
jref patch src/main.ts updated-snapshot.json > patched.json
```

### Summarize (Architectural Map)

```bash
# Generate a token-efficient map (strips function bodies)
jref summarize project.json > map.json

# Pipe from stdin
cat project.json | jref summarize > map.json

# Summarize outputs a new valid snapshot with implementation details
# stripped — you can pipe it back through other jref commands
cat project.json | jref summarize | jref inspect --files
```

### Serve as MCP Server

```bash
# Start stdio MCP server (blocks)
jref serve project.json
```

The MCP server exposes three tools over stdio using the Model Context Protocol:

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `inspect` | Returns snapshot metadata + directory structure as JSON |
| `search` | Searches files matching a regex pattern, returns matching file paths |
| `query` | Returns content of a specific file path |

This enables AI agents (Claude Code, Codex, etc.) to interactively browse and query the snapshot through their native MCP integration.

### Interactive TUI Browser

```bash
# Start the interactive browser
jref ui project.json
```

**Keyboard controls:**

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `↑` / `k` | Move up |
| `↓` / `j` | Move down |
| `←` / `h` | Collapse directory |
| `→` / `l` | Expand directory |
| `Enter` | Select / enter directory / open file |
| `/` | Open search mode |
| `y` | Yank (copy) file content to clipboard |
| `e` | Edit file in `$EDITOR` (in-memory, does not write to disk) |
| `c` | Toggle compact mode (for narrow terminals) |
| `Esc` | Back / Exit |

**Mobile/Termux optimizations:**
- Compact mode auto-detected when terminal width < 60 columns
- Yank uses `termux-clipboard-set` on Termux, `pbcopy` on macOS, `xclip` on Linux
- Edit spawns `$EDITOR` (default: `vi`) for temporary in-memory edits

---

## Global Options

These flags work with **any** command and must appear before the command name:

```
jref [--json|-j] [--silent|-s] [--raw|-r] [--help|-h] [--version|-v] <command> [args...]
```

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `--json`, `-j` | Emit JSON instead of human-readable text. Every command supports this. |
| `--silent`, `-s` | Suppress all ASCII art, progress indicators, and decorative output. |
| `--raw`, `-r` | Emit raw output with no formatting. For `--query`, this means pure file content without headers. For AI agents consuming file content. |
| `--help`, `-h` | Show global help (all commands). Append to any command for command-specific help: `jref pack --help` |
| `--version`, `-v` | Print `jref v1.0.0` (or `--json` for `{"version": "1.0.0"}`). |

---

## Command Reference

---

### pack

Create a jref-compliant JSON snapshot from a local directory.

```
jref pack [directory] [options]
```

**Options:**

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `--instruction <text>` | Add an `instruction` field to the snapshot — AI system prompt context |
| `--summary <text>` | Add a `fileSummary` field — human-readable project overview |

**Behavior:**
- Defaults to current working directory if no directory is given
- Reads `.gitignore` from the root of the target directory and respects its patterns
- Always ignores `.git`
- Reads **all file contents as UTF-8 strings**
- Generates an ASCII tree representation for `directoryStructure`
- Non-code files (images, binaries) are stored as empty strings (or skipped if unreadable)

**Examples:**

```bash
# Snapshot current directory
jref pack . > snapshot.json

# Snapshot src/ with instructions
jref pack ./src --instruction "This module follows TDD" > src.json

# Snapshot entire project with summary
jref pack . --summary "Full-stack app: React frontend, Express backend" > full.json
```

---

### inspect

View `directoryStructure` and metadata **without loading the entire file into memory**. Supports streaming for large snapshots.

```
jref inspect [options] [file]
```

**Options:**

| Option | Alias | Description |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| `--metadata` | `-m` | Show only snapshot metadata (file count, total size, etc.) |
| `--structure` | `-s` | Show only the ASCII directory tree |
| `--files` | `-f` | Show only the file list with sizes |
| `--summary` | | Show `instruction`, `fileSummary`, and `userProvidedHeader` fields |

Without any flag, all sections are displayed.

**Output sections:**

- **Snapshot Metadata** — file count, total byte size, number of tree lines, presence of instruction/summary/header
- **Directory Structure** — the ASCII tree string
- **File List** — all file paths with human-readable sizes
- **Summary** — custom header, file summary, and AI instructions

**Examples:**

```bash
jref inspect snapshot.json
jref inspect --metadata snapshot.json
jref inspect --structure snapshot.json
jref inspect --files snapshot.json
jref inspect --summary snapshot.json
cat snapshot.json | jref inspect --json
```

---

### search

High-speed regex or literal keyword search across all file contents in a snapshot.

```
jref search <pattern> [options] [file]
```

**Options:**

| Option | Alias | Description |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| `--regex` | `-r` | Treat `<pattern>` as a regular expression (default: literal string) |
| `--case-insensitive` | `-i` | Case-insensitive match |
| `--files` | `-f` | Output only file paths that contain matches |
| `--max-results` | `-n` | Maximum number of files to return (default: 1000) |
| `--context` | `-c` | Number of context lines around each match (default: 0) |

**Output:** For each matching file, shows:
- File path and match count
- Up to 5 matching lines with highlighted match text (ANSI yellow)
- Remaining match count if > 5

**Examples:**

```bash
jref search "async" snapshot.json
jref search "class.*extends" --regex snapshot.json
jref search "TODO" --case-insensitive snapshot.json
jref search "export" --max-results 10 snapshot.json
jref search "function" --context 2 snapshot.json
cat snapshot.json | jref search "TODO"
```

---

### query

Retrieve the raw content of a specific file path from the snapshot. Designed for AI agent workflows.

```
jref query --path <path> [file]
```

**Options:**

| Option | Alias | Description |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| `--path <path>` | `-p` | **Required.** Path of the file to retrieve. |
| `--raw` | `-r` | Emit pure file content (no header, no formatting). Ideal for piping. |
| `--line-start <n>` | | Start reading from line `n` (1-indexed) |
| `--line-end <n>` | | Stop reading at line `n` (1-indexed, inclusive) |

**Examples:**

```bash
# Human-readable with header
jref query --path "src/main.ts" snapshot.json

# Pure content for AI parsing
jref query --path "src/main.ts" --raw snapshot.json

# Pipe query output to patch another snapshot
jref query --path "src/utils.ts" --raw snapshot.json | \
  jref patch src/utils.ts snapshot.json > updated.json

# Line range
jref query --path "src/main.ts" --line-start 10 --line-end 50 snapshot.json

# JSON structured output
jref query --path "src/main.ts" --json snapshot.json
```

---

### extract

Unpack files from a snapshot back into the local filesystem.

```
jref extract [options] [file]
```

**Options:**

| Option | Alias | Description |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| `--output <dir>` | `-o` | Output directory (default: `./extracted`) |
| `--paths <paths...>` | `-p` | Extract only these specific file paths |
| `--overwrite` | `-w` | Overwrite existing files on disk |
| `--dry-run` | `-n` | Preview what would be extracted without writing files |
| `--flat` | | Flatten: extract filenames only (no directory structure) |

**Examples:**

```bash
# Extract entire project
jref extract snapshot.json

# Extract to custom directory
jref extract --output ./restored snapshot.json

# Extract specific files
jref extract --paths src/main.ts src/utils.ts snapshot.json

# Flatten output (all files side by side)
jref extract --flat --output ./flat snapshot.json

# Dry run preview
jref extract --dry-run snapshot.json

# Overwrite existing
jref extract --overwrite snapshot.json
```

---

### reconstruct

Dry-run verification: checks whether a local directory's contents match the snapshot's expected state. Reports missing, extra, and modified files.

```
jref reconstruct [options] [file]
```

**Options:**

| Option | Alias | Description |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| `--directory <dir>` | `-d` | Local directory to compare against (default: `.`) |
| `--verbose` | `-v` | List the actual missing/extra/modified file names |
| `--ignore-missing` | | Only check for extra/modified, ignore missing files |
| `--ignore-extra` | | Only check for missing/modified, ignore extra local files |

**Exit codes:** `0` = perfect match, `1` = differences found.

**Files ignored during comparison:** `node_modules`, `.git`, `.svn`, `.hg`, `__pycache__`, `.DS_Store`, `Thumbs.db`, `.env`, `.env.local`, `dist`, `build`, `.cache`

**Examples:**

```bash
# Check if . matches the snapshot
jref reconstruct snapshot.json

# Check specific directory
jref reconstruct --directory ./my-app snapshot.json

# Verbose listing of differences
jref reconstruct --verbose snapshot.json

# JSON output for automation
jref reconstruct --json snapshot.json
```

---

### diff

Compare a snapshot against the local filesystem, listing files that differ.

```
jref diff [options] [file]
```

**Options:**

| Option | Alias | Description |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| `--directory <dir>` | `-d` | Target directory to compare against (default: current directory) |
| `--all` | `-a` | Include local files that are **not** in the snapshot in the output |

**Output classification:**

| Status | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| `M` | Modified — content differs between snapshot and disk |
| `A` | Added to snapshot — file exists in snapshot but not on disk |
| `D` | Deleted from snapshot — file exists on disk but not in snapshot |

**Examples:**

```bash
jref diff snapshot.json
jref diff --directory ./staging snapshot.json
jref diff --all snapshot.json
jref diff --json snapshot.json
```

---

### patch

Update or add file contents and/or metadata in a snapshot **without extracting it to disk**. Supports stdin content piping. The canonical way to surgically mutate a snapshot.

```
jref patch [path] [content] [file.json]
```

**Options:**

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `--instruction <text>` | Set/update the `instruction` field |
| `--summary <text>` | Set/update the `fileSummary` field |

**Positional argument rules:**

| Args given | Interpretation |
|------------|----------------|
| `path` | `path` is the file to update; content comes from **stdin** |
| `path content` | `path` is the file to update; `content` is the new content string |
| `path file.json` | `path` is the file; `file.json` is the snapshot file path (no inline content) |
| `path content file.json` | All three explicitly provided |
| `file.json` (ends in `.json`) | Metadata-only update of that snapshot file |

**Stdin behavior:** If `content` is not provided as a positional arg and stdin is piped, the **entire stdin content** is used as the file's new content.

**Examples:**

```bash
# Content from argument
jref patch src/main.ts "const x = 1;" snapshot.json > updated.json

# Content from stdin
cat new-main.ts | jref patch src/main.ts snapshot.json > updated.json

# Update metadata only
jref patch --instruction "Follow the new style guide" snapshot.json > updated.json

# Update both content and metadata
cat new-auth.ts | jref patch src/auth.ts \
  --instruction "Auth module updated with OAuth2" \
  snapshot.json > updated.json

# Auto-detect: second positional ending in .json is the snapshot file
jref patch src/main.ts updated-snapshot.json > patched.json
```

---

### summarize

Generate a **token-efficient architectural map** by stripping implementation details from code files while preserving:
- All `import` / `export` statements
- All `function` / `class` / `interface` / `type` declarations
- All decorator lines (`@` prefix)
- Blank lines

Non-code files (`.json`, `.md`, `.txt`) are kept verbatim.

The output is a **valid snapshot** that can be piped back into other jref commands.

**Primary use case:** Injecting a compact overview of a large codebase into an LLM's context window without burning tokens on implementation details.

```
jref summarize [file]
```

**Examples:**

```bash
# Generate architectural map
jref summarize snapshot.json > map.json

# Chain: summarize then inspect only file list
cat snapshot.json | jref summarize | jref inspect --files

# JSON output
jref summarize --json snapshot.json
```

**Stripping behavior:**

| Line type | Kept? |
|-----------|-------|
| `import ...` | ✅ |
| `export ...` | ✅ |
| `function foo(...)` | ✅ (body stripped) |
| `class Foo ...` | ✅ (body stripped) |
| `interface ...` | ✅ |
| `type ...` | ✅ |
| `@decorator` | ✅ |
| Everything else | ❌ (replaced with `/* ... implementation details stripped ... */`) |

---

### serve

Start a **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** stdio server that exposes snapshot tools to AI agents. The server reads the snapshot file once at startup and serves requests over stdio using the MCP JSON-RPC protocol.

```
jref serve <file>
```

**MCP Tools exposed:**

| Tool | Input | Output |
|------|-------|--------|
| `inspect` | (none) | JSON with `metadata` and `directoryStructure` |
| `search` | `{ pattern: string }` | JSON array of file paths containing the pattern |
| `query` | `{ path: string }` | Raw file content as plain text |

**Examples:**

```bash
# Block and serve (use with agent runner)
jref serve snapshot.json
```

**Integration:** Connect the stdio transport to any MCP-compatible agent runner (Claude Code, etc.):

```bash
# Example: connect to Claude Code's MCP
claude --mcp "jref serve snapshot.json"
```

---

### ui

Interactive Terminal User Interface (TUI) for visually browsing a snapshot's directory tree and file contents. Built with Ink (React for CLI).

```
jref ui [file]
```

**Launch:** `jref ui snapshot.json`

**Two-pane layout:** The left/top shows the directory tree; selecting a file shows its content in the right/bottom pane.

**Controls:**

| Key | Tree View | File View | Search Mode |
|-----|-----------|-----------|-------------|
| `↑` / `k` | Move up | Scroll up | Move up |
| `↓` / `j` | Move down | Scroll down | Move down |
| `←` / `h` | Collapse dir | — | — |
| `→` / `l` | Expand dir | — | — |
| `Enter` | Select/toggle | Back to tree | Select file |
| `/` | Open search | — | — |
| `y` | Yank content | Yank content | — |
| `e` | Edit in `$EDITOR` | — | — |
| `c` | Toggle compact | Toggle compact | — |
| `Esc` | Exit | Back to tree | Cancel search |

**Yank behavior:**
- Termux: uses `termux-clipboard-set`
- macOS: uses `pbcopy`
- Linux: uses `xclip -selection clipboard`

**Edit behavior:** Spawns `$EDITOR` (default `vi`) with the file content in a temp file. On exit, the in-memory file is updated — **changes are not written back to the snapshot or the filesystem**.

**Compact mode:** Auto-enabled when terminal width < 60 columns. Shows abbreviated UI elements to fit narrow screens.

---

## Snapshot Schema

A valid jref snapshot is a JSON object conforming to this schema:

```json
{
  "directoryStructure": "ASCII tree string (required)",
  "files": {
    "path/to/file1.ts": "file content string (required)",
    "path/to/file2.py": "file content string"
  },
  "instruction": "AI system prompt context (optional)",
  "fileSummary": "Human-readable project overview (optional)",
  "userProvidedHeader": "Custom header note (optional)"
}
```

### Field Descriptions

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `directoryStructure` | `string` | ✅ Yes | ASCII tree representation of the directory layout |
| `files` | `object` | ✅ Yes | Map of relative file paths to their UTF-8 content strings |
| `instruction` | `string` | No | AI-specific instructions or context injected at pack time |
| `fileSummary` | `string` | No | Human-readable description of the project structure |
| `userProvidedHeader` | `string` | No | Arbitrary custom header or metadata note |

---

## AI Agent Workflows

### Context Injection

```bash
# Get a compact architectural map for initial planning
jref summarize project.json > map.json

# Inspect the summarized map
jref inspect --files map.json
```

### State Mutation

```bash
# AI suggests a fix; apply it directly to the snapshot
cat fix.ts | jref patch src/main.ts project.json > updated.json

# Chain: query → patch
jref query --path "src/utils.ts" --raw project.json | \
  sed 's/old/new/g' | \
  jref patch src/utils.ts project.json > updated.json
```

### Tool Interoperability

```bash
# Start MCP server for agent-native browsing
jref serve project.json
```

### Verification Loop

```bash
# After applying edits locally, verify against snapshot
jref reconstruct --verbose project.json

# Or diff to see all differences
jref diff --all project.json
```

### Streaming Large Snapshots

```bash
# For very large snapshots (>8MB), jref uses chunked streaming
# to avoid heap overflow on low-memory devices (Raspberry Pi, Termux)
# No special flag needed — handled automatically
jref inspect large-snapshot.json
```

---

## Target Environments

jref is engineered for constrained computing environments:

| Environment | Notes |
|-------------|-------|
| **Termux (Android)** | Full clipboard integration via `termux-clipboard-set`; ARM-compatible |
| **Raspberry Pi 4 (ARM)** | Chunked streaming for large snapshots; low memory footprint |
| **Standard Linux (x86)** | Full feature set |
| **macOS** | Full feature set; `pbcopy` for clipboard |

### Memory Management

- Snapshots **< 8MB**: loaded directly via `JSON.parse`
- Snapshots **≥ 8MB**: streamed in 64KB chunks to prevent heap overflow
- The TUI auto-activates compact mode on narrow terminals (< 60 columns)

---

## Use Cases

### 1. Code Review by AI Agent
```bash
jref pack ./project > snapshot.json
jref summarize snapshot.json > map.json
# Inject map.json into LLM context for architectural overview
# Use jref query for targeted file reads during review
```

### 2. Portable Development Context
```bash
# Create a shareable snapshot of a project
jref pack . --instruction "This is a React dashboard" > context.json

# Share the single JSON file instead of a zip
# Recipient: jref inspect context.json
```

### 3. Surgical Patch without Full Extraction
```bash
# Fix a bug in a file inside a snapshot without unpacking everything
cat fixed.ts | jref patch src/buggy.ts snapshot.json > fixed.json
```

### 4. Backup / Audit Trail
```bash
# Snapshot a project state
jref pack . --instruction "State at commit abc123" > state-abc123.json
# Store the JSON as an immutable artifact
```

### 5. CI/CD Verification
```bash
# In CI: ensure deployed code matches the approved snapshot
jref diff --all snapshot.json && echo "Match" || echo "Drift detected"
```

---

## Exit Codes

All jref commands return an exit code:

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `0` | Success |
| `1` | General error (file not found, invalid input, etc.) |
| `2` | Query target file not found in snapshot |
| MCP server | Runs indefinitely; no exit unless connection drops |

---

## File Size and Streaming Behavior

jref handles JSON snapshot files of any size through automatic streaming:

| Snapshot Size | Strategy |
|--------------|----------|
| < 8 MB | Direct `JSON.parse` — fast, single allocation |
| ≥ 8 MB | 64KB chunked read + streaming parse — prevents OOM |
| > ~16 MB | Returns error (`"Snapshot file too large to process safely"`) |

The threshold is configurable internally via `MAX_BUFFER_SIZE` in `streaming-json.js` (currently 8MB).

.SH DESCRIPTION
.B jref
(JSON Reference) is a CLI tool for creating, inspecting, searching,
and manipulating condensed JSON project snapshots. A snapshot is a
single JSON file that encodes an entire codebase's directory tree and
all file contents, along with optional AI instructions and project
metadata.
.PP
Snapshots are designed to be:
.IP \(em 2
Portable — a single file instead of a directory of files
.IP \(em 2
Streamable — large snapshots (>8MB) are processed in 64KB chunks to
avoid memory exhaustion on mobile/ARM devices
.IP \(em 2
AI-friendly — structured JSON output, stdin/stdout pipe support, and
an MCP server mode for agentic interoperability
.IP \(em 2
Human-friendly — an interactive TUI browser, colored output, and
human-readable tree visualization
.PP
All commands accept input from stdin via pipe, e.g.,
.BR "cat snapshot.json | jref inspect" .
.
.
.SH GLOBAL OPTIONS
These flags apply to any command and must appear before the command name.
.TP
.B \-\-json\fR, \fB\-j
Output structured JSON instead of human-readable text. Every command
supports this flag.
.TP
.B \-\-silent\fR, \fB\-s
Suppress all decorative output, progress indicators, and ASCII art
headers.
.TP
.B \-\-raw\fR, \fB\-r
Raw output mode. Suppresses all formatting. For
.BR query ,
this emits pure file content without headers or labels — ideal for
piping into other commands or passing directly to an AI.
.TP
.B \-\-help\fR, \fB\-h
Show the global help listing all commands. Append to any specific
command for command-specific help, e.g.,
.BR "jref pack \-\-help" .
.TP
.B \-\-version\fR, \fB\-v
Print the version string
.BR "jref v1.0.0" .
Use with
.B \-\-json
to get
.BR "{\"version\": \"1.0.0\"}" .
.
.
.SH COMMANDS
.
.SS PACK
Create a jref snapshot from a local directory.
.PP
.B SYNOPSIS
.RS 4
jref pack [directory] [options]
.RE
.PP
.B OPTIONS
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B \-\-instruction \fItext\fR
Set the
.BR instruction
field in the snapshot — AI system prompt context embedded in the
snapshot.
.TP
.B \-\-summary \fItext\fR
Set the
.BR fileSummary
field — a human-readable description of the project.
.RE
.PP
.B DESCRIPTION
Reads
.I directory
(recursively) and produces a JSON snapshot. If
.I directory
is omitted, defaults to the current working directory.
.PP
Reads
.B .gitignore
from the root of
.I directory
and respects its patterns; the
.B .git
directory is always excluded. All file contents are read as UTF-8
strings.
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
jref pack . > snapshot.json
jref pack ./src \e
  --instruction "Follow TDD" > src.json
.EE
.
.SS INSPECT
View snapshot metadata and directory tree without loading the entire
file into memory.
.PP
.B SYNOPSIS
.RS 4
jref inspect [options] [file]
.RE
.PP
.B OPTIONS
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B \-\-metadata\fR, \fB\-m
Show only snapshot metadata (file count, total byte size, directory
structure line count, presence of instruction/summary/header fields).
.TP
.B \-\-structure\fR, \fB\-s
Show only the ASCII directory tree.
.TP
.B \-\-files\fR, \fB\-f
Show only the list of file paths with human-readable sizes.
.TP
.B \-\-summary
Show the contents of the
.BR instruction ,
.BR fileSummary ,
and
.BR userProvidedHeader
fields, if present.
.RE
.PP
If no option flags are given, all sections are displayed.
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
jref inspect snapshot.json
jref inspect --metadata snapshot.json
jref inspect --structure snapshot.json
cat snapshot.json | jref inspect --json
.EE
.
.SS SEARCH
Search file contents within a snapshot using literal keyword matching
or regular expressions.
.PP
.B SYNOPSIS
.RS 4
jref search \fIpattern\fR [options] [file]
.RE
.PP
.B OPTIONS
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B \-\-regex\fR, \fB\-r
Treat
.I pattern
as a regular expression (default: literal string, with regex
metacharacters escaped automatically).
.TP
.B \-\-case\-insensitive\fR, \fB\-i
Case-insensitive matching.
.TP
.B \-\-files\fR, \fB\-f
Output only the file paths that contain matches, not the matching
lines themselves.
.TP
.B \-\-max\-results\fR, \fB\-n \fIn\fR
Maximum number of files to return (default: 1000).
.TP
.B \-\-context\fR, \fB\-c \fIn\fR
Show
.I n
lines of context around each match (default: 0).
.RE
.PP
.B OUTPUT
For each matching file:
.IP \(em 2
File path and total match count
.IP \(em 2
Up to 5 matching lines with the match text highlighted in ANSI yellow
.IP \(em 2
Count of remaining matches if more than 5
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
jref search "async function" snapshot.json
jref search "class.*Controller" --regex snapshot.json
jref search "TODO" --case-insensitive snapshot.json
jref search "function" --context 2 snapshot.json
cat snapshot.json | jref search "TODO"
.EE
.
.SS QUERY
Retrieve the content of a specific file path from the snapshot. The
primary interface for AI agents reading individual files from a
snapshot.
.PP
.B SYNOPSIS
.RS 4
jref query --path \fIpath\fR [file]
.RE
.PP
.B OPTIONS
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B \-\-path \fIpath\fR, \fB\-p \fIpath\fR
\fBRequired.\fR The relative path of the file to retrieve from the
snapshot.
.TP
.B \-\-raw\fR, \fB\-r
Emit only the raw file content, with no header, formatting, or label.
This is the ideal mode for piping output to other commands or passing
content to an AI.
.TP
.B \-\-line-start \fIn\fR
Start reading from line
.I n
(1-indexed, inclusive).
.TP
.B \-\-line-end \fIn\fR
Stop reading at line
.I n
(1-indexed, inclusive).
.RE
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
# Human-readable with header
jref query --path "src/main.ts" snapshot.json

# Raw output (pure content, for AI or piping)
jref query --path "src/main.ts" --raw snapshot.json

# Line range
jref query --path "src/main.ts" --line-start 10 --line-end 50 snapshot.json

# Pipe query output into a patch operation
jref query --path "src/utils.ts" --raw snapshot.json \e
  | sed 's/old/new/g' \e
  | jref patch src/utils.ts snapshot.json > updated.json
.EE
.
.SS EXTRACT
Unpack files from a snapshot back into the local filesystem.
.PP
.B SYNOPSIS
.RS 4
jref extract [options] [file]
.RE
.PP
.B OPTIONS
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B \-\-output \fIdir\fR, \fB\-o \fIdir\fR
Destination directory. Defaults to
.B ./extracted
if not specified.
.TP
.B \-\-paths \fIpaths\fR...\fR, \fB\-p \fIpaths\fR...\fR
Extract only the specified file paths. All other files in the
snapshot are ignored.
.TP
.B \-\-overwrite\fR, \fB\-w
Overwrite existing files on disk. By default, existing files are
skipped.
.TP
.B \-\-dry-run\fR, \fB\-n
Preview what would be extracted without writing any files to disk.
.TP
.B \-\-flat
Flatten the output: files are written directly into the output
directory with no subdirectory structure (only the filename is used).
.RE
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
jref extract snapshot.json
jref extract --output ./restored snapshot.json
jref extract --paths src/main.ts src/utils.ts snapshot.json
jref extract --dry-run snapshot.json
jref extract --flat --output ./flat snapshot.json
.EE
.
.SS RECONSTRUCT
Dry-run verification: check whether the contents of a local directory
match the expected state recorded in the snapshot. Reports missing,
extra, and modified files.
.PP
.B SYNOPSIS
.RS 4
jref reconstruct [options] [file]
.RE
.PP
.B OPTIONS
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B \-\-directory \fIdir\fR, \fB\-d \fIdir\fR
Local directory to compare against. Defaults to the current working
directory.
.TP
.B \-\-verbose\fR, \fB\-v
List the actual missing, extra, and modified file paths in detail.
.TP
.B \-\-ignore-missing
Ignore files that are in the snapshot but not on disk.
.TP
.B \-\-ignore-extra
Ignore files that are on disk but not in the snapshot.
.RE
.PP
.B EXIT CODES
.TP 4
0
Directory matches the snapshot perfectly.
.TP 4
1
One or more differences were found.
.PP
.B IGNORED PATHS
The following patterns are always ignored during comparison:
.BR node_modules ,
.BR .git ,
.BR .svn ,
.BR .hg ,
.BR __pycache__ ,
.BR .DS_Store ,
.BR Thumbs.db ,
.BR .env ,
.BR .env.local ,
.BR dist ,
.BR build ,
.BR .cache .
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
jref reconstruct snapshot.json
jref reconstruct --directory ./my-app snapshot.json
jref reconstruct --verbose snapshot.json
jref reconstruct --json snapshot.json
.EE
.
.SS DIFF
Compare a snapshot against the local filesystem, listing files that
differ in content or are present/absent.
.PP
.B SYNOPSIS
.RS 4
jref diff [options] [file]
.RE
.PP
.B OPTIONS
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B \-\-directory \fIdir\fR, \fB\-d \fIdir\fR
Target directory to compare against. Defaults to the current working
directory.
.TP
.B \-\-all\fR, \fB\-a
Also report local files that are not present in the snapshot (extra
files on disk).
.RE
.PP
.B OUTPUT CLASSIFICATION
.TP 4
.B M
Modified — file content differs between snapshot and disk.
.TP 4
.B A
Added to snapshot — file exists in snapshot but not on disk.
.TP 4
.B D
Deleted from snapshot — file exists on disk but not in snapshot.
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
jref diff snapshot.json
jref diff --directory ./staging snapshot.json
jref diff --all snapshot.json
jref diff --json snapshot.json
.EE
.
.SS PATCH
Update or add file contents and/or metadata in a snapshot without
unpacking it to disk. This is the canonical mechanism for surgically
mutating a snapshot.
.PP
.B SYNOPSIS
.RS 4
jref patch [path] [content] [file.json]
.RE
.PP
.B OPTIONS
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B \-\-instruction \fItext\fR
Set or update the snapshot's
.BR instruction
field.
.TP
.B \-\-summary \fItext\fR
Set or update the snapshot's
.BR fileSummary
field.
.RE
.PP
.B POSITIONAL ARGUMENT RULES
.TP 4
.B path
Only the file path is given: the file content is read from
.B stdin
if a pipe is present.
.TP 4
.B path content
Both are given as separate arguments; content is used directly.
.TP 4
.B path file.json
The second positional ending in
.B .json
is interpreted as the snapshot file path, and content is read from
.B stdin
if available.
.TP 4
.B file.json
Only a path ending in
.B .json
is given: performs a metadata-only update (no file content change).
.RE
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
# Content from argument
jref patch src/main.ts "const x = 1;" snapshot.json > updated.json

# Content from stdin
cat new-main.ts | jref patch src/main.ts snapshot.json > updated.json

# Metadata only
jref patch --instruction "Updated rules" snapshot.json > updated.json

# Both content and metadata
cat new-auth.ts | jref patch src/auth.ts \e
  --instruction "Auth module updated" \e
  snapshot.json > updated.json
.EE
.
.SS SUMMARIZE
Generate a token-efficient architectural map by stripping
implementation details from code files. Preserves import/export
statements, function signatures, class/interface declarations, and
decorators. Non-code files
.RB ( .json ,
.BR .md ,
.BR .txt )
are kept verbatim.
.PP
.B SYNOPSIS
.RS 4
jref summarize [file]
.RE
.PP
.B DESCRIPTION
The output is a valid jref snapshot containing only the signatures and
import/export lines of each code file, marked with a trailer comment
.BR "/* ... implementation details stripped ... */" .
This snapshot can be piped back into other
.B jref
commands, making it suitable for injecting a compact overview of a
large codebase into an LLM's context window.
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
jref summarize snapshot.json > map.json
cat snapshot.json | jref summarize | jref inspect --files
.EE
.
.SS SERVE
Start a Model Context Protocol (MCP) stdio server that exposes the
snapshot to AI agents via JSON-RPC over stdio.
.PP
.B SYNOPSIS
.RS 4
jref serve \fIfile\fR
.RE
.PP
.B DESCRIPTION
Loads the snapshot at startup and runs indefinitely, handling JSON-RPC
requests from an MCP client over standard input/output.
.PP
.B MCP TOOLS EXPOSED
.TP 4
.B inspect
Returns snapshot metadata and the full directory structure as JSON.
.TP 4
.B search
Arguments:
.BR { pattern: string }
.RS 4
Returns a JSON array of file paths that contain the pattern.
.RE
.TP 4
.B query
Arguments:
.BR { path: string }
.RS 4
Returns the raw content of the specified file.
.RE
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
# Block and serve; connect to an MCP-compatible agent runner
jref serve snapshot.json
.EE
.
.SS UI
Interactive Terminal User Interface (TUI) for visually browsing a
snapshot's directory tree and file contents. Built with Ink (React for
CLI).
.PP
.B SYNOPSIS
.RS 4
jref ui [file]
.RE
.PP
.B CONTROLS
.TP 4
.B Up/Down arrows
or
.B k/j
Navigate up/down in tree view or search results.
.TP 4
.B Left/Right arrows
or
.B h/l
Collapse (left) or expand (right) a directory.
.TP 4
.B Enter
Select a tree item (open directory or view file).
.TP 4
.B /
Open search mode. Type to filter files by name.
.TP 4
.B y
Yank (copy) the current file's content to the system clipboard.
.IP ""
On Termux: uses
.BR termux-clipboard-set .
.IP ""
On macOS: uses
.BR pbcopy .
.IP ""
On Linux: uses
.BR xclip\ -selection\ clipboard .
.TP 4
.B e
Edit the current file in
.BR $EDITOR .
Spawns the editor with file content in a temporary file. On exit, the
in-memory snapshot is updated; changes are
.B not
written to the snapshot file or the filesystem.
.TP 4
.B c
Toggle compact mode (reduces UI elements for narrow terminals).
.TP 4
.B Esc
Return to previous view (file view \\(-> tree view), or exit the TUI
from tree view.
.PP
.B COMPACT MODE
Automatically activated when terminal width is less than 60 columns.
Displays abbreviated labels and simplified borders.
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
jref ui snapshot.json
cat snapshot.json | jref ui
.EE
.
.
.SH SNAPSHOT SCHEMA
A valid jref snapshot is a JSON object with the following structure:
.PP
.EX
{
  "directoryStructure": "ASCII tree string (required)",
  "files": {
    "path/to/file1.ts": "file content string (required)",
    "path/to/file2.py": "file content string"
  },
  "instruction": "AI system prompt context (optional)",
  "fileSummary": "Human-readable project overview (optional)",
  "userProvidedHeader": "Custom header note (optional)"
}
.EE
.
.
.SH STREAMING AND MEMORY
.B jref
handles JSON snapshots of any size using automatic streaming:
.TP 4
< 8 MB
Loaded directly via
.BR JSON.parse ;
fast, single memory allocation.
.TP 4
\(>= 8 MB
64KB chunked read with streaming parse; prevents heap exhaustion on
low-memory devices.
.TP 4
> ~16 MB
Returns error
.BR "Snapshot file too large to process safely" .
.
.
.SH TARGET ENVIRONMENTS
.B jref
is engineered for constrained computing environments:
.TP 4
Termux (Android/ARM)
Full clipboard integration via
.BR termux-clipboard-set ;
ARM-compatible binary.
.TP 4
Raspberry Pi 4 (ARM)
Chunked streaming for large snapshots; minimal memory footprint.
.TP 4
Standard Linux (x86)
Full feature set.
.TP 4
macOS
Full feature set;
.BR pbcopy
for clipboard integration.
.
.
.SH EXIT CODES
.TP 4
.B 0
Success.
.TP 4
.B 1
General error: file not found, invalid arguments, parsing error, etc.
.TP 4
.B 2
Query target file not found in snapshot.
.PP
Note: the
.B serve
command runs indefinitely until the MCP client disconnects and does
not return an exit code during normal operation.
.
.
.SH EXAMPLES
.SS Full Workflow
.EX
# Create a snapshot
jref pack . --instruction "React dashboard app" > project.json

# Inspect it
jref inspect project.json

# Search for something
jref search "async" project.json

# Extract a specific file
jref extract --paths src/main.tsx project.json

# Verify local disk matches snapshot
jref reconstruct --verbose project.json

# Get an architectural overview for an LLM
jref summarize project.json > map.json

# Start MCP server for agent integration
jref serve project.json
.EE
.
.SS AI Agent Pipelines
.EX
# Query + patch: apply a fix from an LLM to the snapshot
jref query --path "src/main.ts" --raw snapshot.json \e
  | sed 's/oldFn/newFn/g' \e
  | jref patch src/main.ts snapshot.json > fixed.json

# Pipe stdin snapshot through multiple jref operations
cat snapshot.json \e
  | jref search "TODO" \e
  | jref summarize \e
  | jref inspect --files
.EE
.
.
.SH FILES
.TP 4
.B /usr/local/bin/jref
The CLI entry point (installed by
.BR "npm install -g" ).
.TP 4
.B ~/.jref/
Configuration directory (future use).
.TP 4
.B /usr/share/man/man1/jref.1
The preformatted man page (installed by the package).
.
.
.SH SEE ALSO
.BR json (1),
.BR jq (1),
.BR mcp (1)
.PP
The jref project snapshot schema is defined in
.B project-snapshot-schema.json
distributed with this tool.
.
.
.SH BUGS
Report bugs at: <https://github.com/your-org/jref/issues>
.
.
.SH AUTHOR
Written by the jref contributors.
.
.
.SH COPYRIGHT
Copyright \(co 2025 the jref contributors.
.br
License: MIT"}

{"path": "./docs/jref.1", "content": ".\" jref.1 - man page for jref JSON Reference CLI
.\" Generated by Hermes Agent
.TH JREF 1 "2025-04-20" "1.0.0" "jref JSON Reference CLI"
.
.SH NAME
jref \- JSON Reference CLI tool for condensed JSON project snapshots
.
.SH SYNOPSIS
.SY jref
[\fB\-\-json\fR|\fB\-j\fR]
[\fB\-\-silent\fR|\fB\-s\fR]
[\fB\-\-raw\fR|\fB\-r\fR]
[\fB\-\-help\fR|\fB\-h\fR]
[\fB\-\-version\fR|\fB\-v\fR]
.IR command
[\fIarguments\fR...]
.YS
.PP
The following commands are available:
.IR pack ,
.IR inspect ,
.IR search ,
.IR query ,
.IR extract ,
.IR reconstruct ,
.IR diff ,
.IR patch ,
.IR summarize ,
.IR serve ,
.IR ui .
.
.SH DESCRIPTION
.B jref
(JSON Reference) is a CLI tool for creating, inspecting, searching,
and manipulating condensed JSON project snapshots.
A snapshot is a single JSON file containing a directory tree,
all file contents keyed by path, and optional AI instructions or
project metadata.
.PP
Snapshots are designed to be portable, streamable for large files,
and AI-friendly with structured JSON I/O and an MCP server mode.
An interactive Terminal UI is also available for visual browsing.
.PP
All commands accept stdin pipe input, e.g.:
.B "cat snapshot.json | jref inspect" .
.
.SH GLOBAL OPTIONS
These flags apply to any command and must appear before the command
name.
.TP 4
.B \-\-json\fR, \fB\-j
Output structured JSON instead of human-readable text.
Every command supports this.
.TP
.B \-\-silent\fR, \fB\-s
Suppress all decorative output, progress indicators, and ASCII art.
.TP
.B \-\-raw\fR, \fB\-r
Raw output mode.
For the
.B query
command this emits pure file content without headers or labels.
.TP
.B \-\-help\fR, \fB\-h
Show global help listing all commands.
Append to any specific command for command-specific help:
.B "jref pack \-\-help" .
.TP
.B \-\-version\fR, \fB\-v
Print the version string
.B "jref v1.0.0" .
Use with
.B \-\-json
to get
.BR "{\e"version\e": \e"1.0.0\e"}" .
.
.SH COMMANDS
.
.SS PACK
Create a jref snapshot from a local directory.
.PP
.B "jref pack [directory] [\fB\-\-instruction\fR \fItext\fR] [\fB\-\-summary\fR \fItext\fR]"
.PP
.B OPTIONS
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B \-\-instruction \fItext\fR
Set the
.B instruction
field in the snapshot (AI system prompt context).
.TP
.B \-\-summary \fItext\fR
Set the
.B fileSummary
field (human-readable project description).
.RE
.PP
Reads
.I directory
recursively and produces a JSON snapshot.
Defaults to the current working directory if omitted.
Reads
.B .gitignore
from the root of
.I directory
and respects its patterns;
.B .git
is always excluded.
All file contents are read as UTF-8.
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
jref pack . > snapshot.json
jref pack ./src \e
  --instruction "Follow TDD" > src.json
.EE
.
.SS INSPECT
View snapshot metadata and directory tree without loading the entire
file into memory.
.PP
.B "jref inspect [\fIoptions\fR] [\fIfile\fR]"
.PP
.I Options
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B \-\-metadata\fR, \fB\-m
Show only snapshot metadata (file count, byte size, structure line
count, presence of instruction/summary/header).
.TP
.B \-\-structure\fR, \fB\-s
Show only the ASCII directory tree.
.TP
.B \-\-files\fR, \fB\-f
Show only the list of file paths with human-readable sizes.
.TP
.B \-\-summary
Show the contents of the
.BR instruction ,
.BR fileSummary ,
and
.B userProvidedHeader
fields, if present.
.RE
.PP
If no option flags are given, all sections are displayed.
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
jref inspect snapshot.json
jref inspect --metadata snapshot.json
jref inspect --structure snapshot.json
cat snapshot.json | jref inspect --json
.EE
.
.SS SEARCH
Search file contents within a snapshot using literal keyword matching
or regular expressions.
.PP
.B "jref search \fIpattern\fR [\fIoptions\fR] [\fIfile\fR]"
.PP
.I Options
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B \-\-regex\fR, \fB\-r
Treat
.I pattern
as a regular expression.
Default: literal string with regex metacharacters escaped.
.TP
.B \-\-case\-insensitive\fR, \fB\-i
Case-insensitive matching.
.TP
.B \-\-files\fR, \fB\-f
Output only the file paths that contain matches, not the matching
lines.
.TP
.B \-\-max\-results\fR, \fB\-n \fIn\fR
Maximum number of files to return (default: 1000).
.TP
.B \-\-context\fR, \fB\-c \fIn\fR
Show
.I n
lines of context around each match (default: 0).
.RE
.PP
For each matching file: the file path and match count are shown,
along with up to 5 matching lines with the match text highlighted.
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
jref search "async function" snapshot.json
jref search "class.*Controller" --regex snapshot.json
jref search "TODO" --case-insensitive snapshot.json
jref search "function" --context 2 snapshot.json
cat snapshot.json | jref search "TODO"
.EE
.
.SS QUERY
Retrieve the content of a specific file path from the snapshot.
The primary interface for AI agents reading individual files.
.PP
.B "jref query \fB\-\-path\fR \fIpath\fR [\fIfile\fR] [\fIoptions\fR]"
.PP
.I Options
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B \-\-path \fIpath\fR, \fB\-p \fIpath\fR
\fBRequired.\fR
The relative path of the file to retrieve from the snapshot.
.TP
.B \-\-raw\fR, \fB\-r
Emit only the raw file content, with no header or formatting.
Ideal for piping to other commands or passing content to an AI.
.TP
.B \-\-line-start \fIn\fR
Start reading from line
.I n
(1-indexed, inclusive).
.TP
.B \-\-line-end \fIn\fR
Stop reading at line
.I n
(1-indexed, inclusive).
.RE
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
# Human-readable with header
jref query --path "src/main.ts" snapshot.json

# Raw output (pure content, for AI or piping)
jref query --path "src/main.ts" --raw snapshot.json

# Line range
jref query --path "src/main.ts" --line-start 10 --line-end 50 snapshot.json

# Pipe query output into a patch operation
jref query --path "src/utils.ts" --raw snapshot.json \e
  | sed 's/old/new/g' \e
  | jref patch src/utils.ts snapshot.json > updated.json
.EE
.
.SS EXTRACT
Unpack files from a snapshot back into the local filesystem.
.PP
.B "jref extract [\fIoptions\fR] [\fIfile\fR]"
.PP
.I Options
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B \-\-output \fIdir\fR, \fB\-o \fIdir\fR
Destination directory.
Defaults to
.B ./extracted
if not specified.
.TP
.B \-\-paths \fIpaths\fR\fB...\fR, \fB\-p \fIpaths\fR\fB...\fR
Extract only the specified file paths.
All other files in the snapshot are ignored.
.TP
.B \-\-overwrite\fR, \fB\-w
Overwrite existing files on disk.
By default, existing files are skipped.
.TP
.B \-\-dry-run\fR, \fB\-n
Preview what would be extracted without writing any files.
.TP
.B \-\-flat
Flatten output: files are written directly into the output
directory with no subdirectory structure.
.RE
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
jref extract snapshot.json
jref extract --output ./restored snapshot.json
jref extract --paths src/main.ts src/utils.ts snapshot.json
jref extract --dry-run snapshot.json
jref extract --flat --output ./flat snapshot.json
.EE
.
.SS RECONSTRUCT
Dry-run verification: check whether a local directory's contents
match the expected state in the snapshot.
Reports missing, extra, and modified files.
.PP
.B "jref reconstruct [\fIoptions\fR] [\fIfile\fR]"
.PP
.I Options
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B \-\-directory \fIdir\fR, \fB\-d \fIdir\fR
Local directory to compare against.
Defaults to the current working directory.
.TP
.B \-\-verbose\fR, \fB\-v
List the actual missing, extra, and modified file paths in detail.
.TP
.B \-\-ignore-missing
Ignore files that are in the snapshot but not on disk.
.TP
.B \-\-ignore-extra
Ignore files that are on disk but not in the snapshot.
.RE
.PP
.B EXIT CODES
.RS 4
.TP 4
0
Directory matches the snapshot perfectly.
.TP 4
1
One or more differences were found.
.RE
.PP
The following patterns are always ignored during comparison:
.BR node_modules ,
.BR .git ,
.BR .svn ,
.BR .hg ,
.BR __pycache__ ,
.BR .DS_Store ,
.BR Thumbs.db ,
.BR .env ,
.BR .env.local ,
.BR dist ,
.BR build ,
.BR .cache .
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
jref reconstruct snapshot.json
jref reconstruct --directory ./my-app snapshot.json
jref reconstruct --verbose snapshot.json
jref reconstruct --json snapshot.json
.EE
.
.SS DIFF
Compare a snapshot against the local filesystem, listing files that
differ in content or are present/absent.
.PP
.B "jref diff [\fIoptions\fR] [\fIfile\fR]"
.PP
.I Options
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B \-\-directory \fIdir\fR, \fB\-d \fIdir\fR
Target directory to compare against.
Defaults to the current working directory.
.TP
.B \-\-all\fR, \fB\-a
Also report local files that are not present in the snapshot.
.RE
.PP
.B OUTPUT CLASSIFICATION
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B M
Modified: file content differs between snapshot and disk.
.TP 4
.B A
Added to snapshot: file exists in snapshot but not on disk.
.TP 4
.B D
Deleted from snapshot: file exists on disk but not in snapshot.
.RE
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
jref diff snapshot.json
jref diff --directory ./staging snapshot.json
jref diff --all snapshot.json
jref diff --json snapshot.json
.EE
.
.SS PATCH
Update or add file contents and/or metadata in a snapshot without
unpacking it to disk.
The canonical mechanism for surgically mutating a snapshot.
.PP
.B "jref patch [\fIpath\fR] [\fIcontent\fR] [\fIfile.json\fR] [\fIoptions\fR]"
.PP
.I Options
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B \-\-instruction \fItext\fR
Set or update the snapshot's
.B instruction
field.
.TP
.B \-\-summary \fItext\fR
Set or update the snapshot's
.B fileSummary
field.
.RE
.PP
.B POSITIONAL ARGUMENT RULES
.RS 4
.TP 4
.I path
only
.RS 4
The file path is given; content is read from
.B stdin
if a pipe is present.
.RE
.TP
.I path content
.RS 4
Both are given as separate arguments; content is used directly.
.RE
.TP
.I path file.json
.RS 4
The second positional ending in
.B .json
is interpreted as the snapshot file path; content is read from
.B stdin
if available.
.RE
.TP
.I file.json
only
.RS 4
A path ending in
.B .json
given alone: performs a metadata-only update.
.RE
.RE
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
# Content from argument
jref patch src/main.ts "const x = 1;" snapshot.json > updated.json

# Content from stdin
cat new-main.ts | jref patch src/main.ts snapshot.json > updated.json

# Metadata only
jref patch --instruction "Updated rules" snapshot.json > updated.json

# Both content and metadata
cat new-auth.ts | jref patch src/auth.ts \e
  --instruction "Auth module updated" \e
  snapshot.json > updated.json
.EE
.
.SS SUMMARIZE
Generate a token-efficient architectural map by stripping
implementation details from code files.
Preserves import/export statements, function signatures,
class/interface/type declarations, and decorators.
Non-code files
.RB ( .json ,
.BR .md ,
.B .txt )
are kept verbatim.
.PP
The output is a valid jref snapshot containing only signatures and
import/export lines, suitable for injecting a compact overview of a
large codebase into an LLM's context window.
.PP
.B "jref summarize [\fIfile\fR]"
.PP
.B EXAMPLES
.PP
.EX
jref summarize snapshot.json > map.json
cat snapshot.json | jref summarize | jref inspect --files
.EE
.
.SS SERVE
Start a Model Context Protocol (MCP) stdio server that exposes the
snapshot to AI agents via JSON-RPC over stdio.
.PP
.B "jref serve \fIfile\fR"
.PP
Loads the snapshot at startup and runs indefinitely, handling
JSON-RPC requests from an MCP client.
.PP
.B MCP TOOLS EXPOSED
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B inspect
Returns snapshot metadata and the full directory structure as JSON.
.TP 4
.B search
Arguments:
.RS 4
{ pattern: string }
.RE
Returns a JSON array of file paths that contain the pattern.
.TP 4
.B query
Arguments:
.RS 4
{ path: string }
.RE
Returns the raw content of the specified file.
.RE
.PP
.B EXAMPLE
.PP
.EX
jref serve snapshot.json
.EE
.
.SS UI
Interactive Terminal User Interface (TUI) for browsing a snapshot's
directory tree and file contents.
Built with Ink (React for CLI).
.PP
.B "jref ui [\fIfile\fR]"
.PP
.B CONTROLS
.RS 4
.TP 4
.B Up/Down
arrows or
.B k/j
.RS 4
Navigate up/down in tree view or search results.
.RE
.TP
.B Left/Right
arrows or
.B h/l
.RS 4
Collapse (left) or expand (right) a directory.
.RE
.TP
.B Enter
Select a tree item (open directory or view file).
.TP
.B /
Open search mode. Type to filter files by name.
.TP
.B y
Yank (copy) the current file's content to the system clipboard.
.RS 4
On Termux: uses
.BR termux-clipboard-set .
.br
On macOS: uses
.BR pbcopy .
.br
On Linux: uses
.BR xclip\ -selection\ clipboard .
.RE
.TP
.B e
Edit the current file in
.BR $EDITOR .
Spawns the editor with file content in a temporary file.
On exit, the in-memory snapshot is updated;
changes are \fBnot\fR written to the snapshot file or filesystem.
.TP
.B c
Toggle compact mode (reduces UI elements for narrow terminals).
Auto-activated when terminal width < 60 columns.
.TP
.B Esc
Return to previous view (file view to tree view), or exit the TUI
from tree view.
.RE
.PP
.B EXAMPLE
.PP
.EX
jref ui snapshot.json
cat snapshot.json | jref ui
.EE
.
.SH SNAPSHOT SCHEMA
A valid jref snapshot is a JSON object with this structure:
.PP
.EX
{
  "directoryStructure": "ASCII tree string (required)",
  "files": {
    "path/to/file1.ts": "file content string (required)",
    "path/to/file2.py": "file content string"
  },
  "instruction": "AI system prompt context (optional)",
  "fileSummary": "Human-readable project overview (optional)",
  "userProvidedHeader": "Custom header note (optional)"
}
.EE
.
.SH STREAMING AND MEMORY
.B jref
handles JSON snapshots of any size using automatic streaming:
.TP 4
< 8 MB
Loaded directly via
.BR JSON.parse ;
single memory allocation.
.TP
>= 8 MB
64KB chunked read with streaming parse; prevents heap exhaustion.
.TP
> ~16 MB
Returns error:
.B "Snapshot file too large to process safely" .
.
.SH TARGET ENVIRONMENTS
.B jref
is engineered for constrained computing environments:
.TP 4
Termux (Android/ARM)
Full clipboard integration via
.BR termux-clipboard-set .
.TP
Raspberry Pi 4 (ARM)
Chunked streaming for large snapshots; minimal memory footprint.
.TP
Standard Linux (x86)
Full feature set.
.TP
macOS
Full feature set;
.BR pbcopy
for clipboard integration.
.
.SH EXIT CODES
.TP 4
.B 0
Success.
.TP
.B 1
General error: file not found, invalid arguments, parse error.
.TP
.B 2
Query target file not found in snapshot.
.PP
The
.B serve
command runs indefinitely until the MCP client disconnects and does
not return an exit code during normal operation.
.
.SH EXAMPLES
.SS Full Workflow
.EX
# Create a snapshot
jref pack . --instruction "React dashboard app" > project.json

# Inspect it
jref inspect project.json

# Search for something
jref search "async" project.json

# Extract a specific file
jref extract --paths src/main.tsx project.json

# Verify local disk matches snapshot
jref reconstruct --verbose project.json

# Get an architectural overview for an LLM
jref summarize project.json > map.json

# Start MCP server for agent integration
jref serve project.json
.EE
.
.SS AI Agent Pipelines
.EX
# Query + patch: apply a fix from an LLM to the snapshot
jref query --path "src/main.ts" --raw snapshot.json \e
  | sed 's/oldFn/newFn/g' \e
  | jref patch src/main.ts snapshot.json > fixed.json

# Pipe stdin snapshot through multiple jref operations
cat snapshot.json \e
  | jref search "TODO" \e
  | jref summarize \e
  | jref inspect --files
.EE
.
.SH FILES
.TP 4
.B /usr/local/bin/jref
CLI entry point (installed by
.B "npm install -g" ).
.TP
.B /usr/local/share/man/man1/jref.1
Man page (standard location on most Linux/macOS systems).
.
.SH SEE ALSO
.BR json (1),
.BR jq (1)
.PP
Project snapshot schema:
.B project-snapshot-schema.json
(distribution package).
.
.SH BUGS
Report bugs at the project issue tracker.
.
.SH COPYRIGHT
Copyright \(co 2025 the jref contributors.
.br
License: MIT"}

Actually, for npm-installed global packages, the standard approach is different. npm doesn't automatically install man pages from the `man` field in package.json when using `npm install -g`. The `man` field in package.json is actually used by npm to look up man pages when running `npm man` or when the package is installed.
 
So the `man` field in package.json does work for global installs—npm will automatically add the man page path to `MANPATH`. I need to place the man page at `{prefix}/share/man/man1/jref.1`, which is what `npm install -g` handles. I'll write a prepack script that does this installation.
